askNext: Which quantisation and LoRA config are you using?
How close is quality to a stronger FT baseline?
Are adapters versioned and reviewed?
qlora.body: QLoRA is thrift stacked on thrift. Measure what thrift costs in quality.
qlora.example: "QLoRA fine-tuned the 70B on one GPU and still needed a week of eval arguments."
qlora.misuse: Assumed identical to full-precision full fine-tuning. Quantisation and adapter limits can reduce quality on hard tasks.
qlora.practicalMeaning: Follow established recipes for rank, quant type, and learning rates; evaluate carefully; watch for training instability.

translations.label: technical meaning
translations.text: QLoRA is a fine-tuning method that freezes a quantised base model (commonly 4-bit) and trains LoRA adapters in higher precision, enabling large-model adaptation on smaller GPUs.

translations.label: what it usually means in reality
translations.text: Follow established recipes for rank, quant type, and learning rates; evaluate carefully; watch for training instability.

qlora.warningLabel: Accessible fine-tuning is still fine-tuning with consequences.
qlora.whyExists: Full fine-tunes of large models are inaccessible to many teams. QLoRA made high-parameter adaptation practical on modest hardware.

askNext: What exact meaning of Quality gate is being used here?
What changes operationally if this label is removed from the sentence?
Is this naming a mechanism, a vendor choice, a risk, or a wish?
quality-gate.body: Quality gate gets its own page because the surrounding discussion around Rubber-Stamp Review keeps needing this sharper distinction. Used well, it turns a vague AI-adjacent gesture into a claim someone can test, budget for, or push back on.
quality-gate.example: "Once quality gate entered the discussion, the team finally had a name for the thing everyone had been gesturing at with less precision."
quality-gate.misuse: It gets abused when people use the phrase as a mood, a shortcut, or a status signal instead of explaining the actual mechanism, incentive, or trade-off they mean.
quality-gate.practicalMeaning: In practice it usually means slowing the conversation down enough to ask whether the label points to a real system property, a commercial incentive, a user behaviour, or just a familiar rhetorical flourish.

translations.label: what it usually means in reality
translations.text: A useful nearby term, provided someone explains the mechanism instead of using it as scenery.

quality-gate.whyExists: It exists because the main term alone is often too blunt. The surrounding conversation needs a way to name the neighbouring idea without pretending it is the same thing.

askNext: Which scheme and bit-width, and calibrated how?
What task metrics regressed?
Is the runtime using real low-precision kernels?
quantisation.body: Quantisation is compression with opinions. Measure the opinions on your workload.
quantisation.example: "Quantisation fit the model on one GPU and made the accounting bot forget how invoices work."
quantisation.misuse: Assumed lossless, or compared across methods without task metrics. A '4-bit model' is not a single quality class.
quantisation.practicalMeaning: Benchmark your tasks after quantisation, watch outliers and long-context behaviour, and pick formats your hardware actually accelerates.

translations.label: technical meaning
translations.text: Quantisation reduces the numeric precision of model weights and/or activations (for example to 8-bit or 4-bit formats) to cut memory and often increase throughput, with some accuracy trade-offs.

translations.label: what it usually means in reality
translations.text: Benchmark your tasks after quantisation, watch outliers and long-context behaviour, and pick formats your hardware actually accelerates.

quantisation.warningLabel: Smaller weights are not automatically the same model.
quantisation.whyExists: Large models strain memory bandwidth and capacity. Lower precision is one of the main practical compression levers for serving.

askNext: Which Qwen model or version are we talking about?
What deployment, licensing, or regional requirements make it attractive?
How does it perform on the actual task, not just on internet reputation?
qwen.body: One healthy sign in AI conversations is when people become more specific as the vendor list widens, not less.
qwen.example: "They were not adopting Qwen as an ideology; they were testing whether a particular variant gave them an acceptable quality-cost trade-off for multilingual support."
qwen.misuse: It gets used as if "Qwen" alone were enough information. In reality, capability, licensing, deployment fit, and tool support depend on the specific model and version. Saying "we benchmarked Qwen" is only the beginning of the sentence.
qwen.note: Newer Qwen references often mean a spread of dense, MoE, coding, multimodal, thinking, and hosted variants. "Which Qwen?" remains the first serious question.
qwen.practicalMeaning: In practice it usually means evaluating one of several model variants for cost, quality, deployment flexibility, coding, agent workflows, multimodal use, or localisation needs. The right comparison is not with a brand in general, but with a task and a constraint set.

translations.label: what it usually means in reality
translations.text: A family of models that still needs task-level evaluation, not a single yes-or-no answer.

qwen.warningLabel: Model family, not a single monolithic model.
qwen.whyExists: The term matters because discussions of AI often collapse into a very small US-centric vendor set. Qwen is one of the model families that reminds people the landscape is broader, more competitive, and more operationally varied than the headlines imply.

askNext: What exact meaning of Qwen model families is being used here?
What changes operationally if this label is removed from the sentence?
Is this naming a mechanism, a vendor choice, a risk, or a wish?
qwen-model-families.body: Qwen model families earns its own page because branded nouns often hide decisions about platform control, pricing, permissions, and operational dependency. This entry keeps the name visible without letting it do the architecture's work.
qwen-model-families.example: "Qwen model families came up in the review, which was useful only after the team clarified whether they meant the product, the platform, the model family, or the vendor relationship."
qwen-model-families.misuse: It gets abused when the name is used as a substitute for specifying the exact product surface, model, API, entitlement, pricing constraint, or operational dependency under discussion.
qwen-model-families.practicalMeaning: In practice it usually means someone should ask what is being bought, integrated, routed through, depended on, or compared before the logo is allowed to stand in for the architecture.

translations.label: procurement meaning
translations.text: Treat the name as a dependency to inspect, not a badge to admire.

qwen-model-families.warningLabel: A brand-shaped noun is not a technical specification.
qwen-model-families.whyExists: The term matters because discussions around Qwen often need to separate the actual technical choice from the brand, distribution channel, contract, or ecosystem wrapped around it.
